displayTitle: Four Ways to Take It Back Off
kicker: PT LEVEL I // CHARACTERISTICS OF EACH METHOD

beats.narration: After the dwell, penetrant sits in two places: a film on the surface, a trace inside the flaw. Removal must take the first, not the second.

payload.title: THE REMOVAL STEP
payload.sub: Where penetrant sensitivity is won or lost.

beats.narration: Four methods solve that problem differently. Each has its own sensitivity ceiling, cost, and one classic mistake that flushes the flaw and kills the test.

payload.heading: One goal, four removal strategies
payload.note: A WASH · B & D EMULSIFY · C WIPE — KEEP IT IN THE FLAW

beats.narration: Method A is water-washable: emulsifier built in, one coarse rinse. Cheap and fast, but the flaw rinses as easily as the surface. Over-washing strips it.
Post-emulsifiable splits the job. Lipophilic diffuses on timed contact; hydrophilic detergent scrubs only the surface, so it owns top sensitivity. Solvent wipes, never floods.

payload.heading: B, D and C — buying back sensitivity

chips.s: Too long diffuses into the flaw

beats.narration: So which is best? Wrong question. The method you can actually control beats the fancier one you cannot. Run yours inside its limits, daily.

payload.sub: The method you can control beats the one you cannot.
